questions.question: What is the primary function of a Look-Up Table (LUT) in a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)?
options: To store large blocks of static random-access memory (SRAM)
To provide high-speed serial communication interfaces
To implement configurable combinational logic functions based on input signals
To manage the clock signals and distribution across the FPGA fabric
To perform analog-to-digital conversion for external sensor inputs

questions.question: In the context of processor microarchitecture, what does 'speculative execution' primarily refer to?
options: Executing instructions only after all preceding instructions have committed their results
Performing arithmetic and logic operations in a dedicated mathematical coprocessor
Executing instructions before the outcome of a preceding branch instruction is definitively known
Storing frequently accessed data in a small, fast memory directly within the CPU core
Prioritizing real-time operating system tasks over user applications

questions.question: What is the main purpose of 'Logic Equivalence Checking' (LEC) in the ASIC design flow?
options: To verify that the physical layout meets all manufacturing design rules (DRC)
To compare two netlists (e.g., RTL vs. gate-level) to confirm they implement the identical logical function
To extract parasitic resistances and capacitances from the final physical layout
To ensure that all timing requirements (setup/hold) are met after routing
To determine the power consumption of the design under various operating conditions

questions.question: In a General-Purpose Graphics Processing Unit (GPGPU) architecture, what does the Single Instruction, Multiple Thread (SIMT) execution model primarily imply?
options: A single CPU core controls multiple independent GPU cores, each running a distinct program
Each thread executes its own unique instruction stream on a private set of data elements
A single instruction stream is broadcast and executed concurrently by multiple processing elements, each operating on different data
All threads within a 'warp' or 'wavefront' execute entirely independently without any form of synchronization
The GPU is optimized for executing a single, very large data stream through a highly complex, variable-length instruction set

questions.question: When designing a low-power digital IC, which technique involves partitioning the chip into separate power domains and using isolation cells to prevent current leakage and logic contention when specific domains are powered down?
options: Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS)
Adaptive Body Biasing (ABB)
Clock Gating
Power Gating
Multi-threshold CMOS (MTCMOS)
